cycles.name: Statistical Abilities

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T21:03:28Z):
> Determinism trap in `run()`: the implementation note says "sort stably" but the acceptance criteria require BOTH a "deterministic same-day tiebreak" AND that "shuffling input row order yields identical final ratings." These conflict. A stable sort on `DATE` alone preserves the *input* relative order for rows sharing a date — so under a shuffled input, same-day matches are processed in a different order, and since Elo updates are path-dependent (`R' = R + K·G·(W−We)`), the final ratings diverge and the shuffle-determinism test fails. martj42 `results.csv` has many same-day fixtures, so this is hit in practice, not a corner case.
> 
> Fix: sort on a composite deterministic key that does not depend on row order, e.g. `df.assign(_d=pd.to_datetime(df["DATE"], errors="coerce")).sort_values(["_d", "HOME_TEAM", "AWAY_TEAM"], kind="stable")`. Add this tiebreak explicitly to the `run` implementation note and assert it in the shuffle test (build a DataFrame with ≥2 matches on the same date involving overlapping teams, then shuffle and compare final ratings). Note also that `pd.to_datetime(errors="coerce")` yields `NaT` for unparseable dates and pandas sorts `NaT` last — decide whether such rows should be dropped or trailed, and cover it.
> 
> Everything else checks out against the code: the `Martj42Loader.load_results()` schema you describe is accurate (`DATE` object, `HOME_GOALS`/`AWAY_GOALS` nullable `Int64`, `NEUTRAL` numpy bool, teams already crosswalk-normalized), the K-tier substring precedence (qualifier before world-cup so "FIFA World Cup qualification" doesn't match the WC tier) is correctly specified, and the additive `config.py` edit fits the existing `AppConfig` pattern.
issues.title: feat: World Football Elo engine (chronological ratings + pre-match Elo-diff from martj42)

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T21:03:29Z):
> Numerical-stability bug risk in the weighted MLE: the Dixon-Coles τ correction is not guaranteed positive during optimization. `τ(0,0) = 1 − λ_home·λ_away·ρ` and the (0,1)/(1,0)/(1,1) cases can each go ≤ 0 as L-BFGS-B explores large λ or large |ρ|. Once τ ≤ 0, `log(τ)` in `_neg_log_likelihood` returns `nan`/`-inf`, which makes the objective non-finite and lets L-BFGS-B either stall or return garbage — silently breaking both the "fit is deterministic" and "stronger team gets higher attack" acceptance criteria. This is the classic Dixon-Coles pitfall and won't show up on tiny toy data but will on realistic fits.
> 
> Fix: constrain ρ during the fit. Pass `bounds=` to `scipy.optimize.minimize(method="L-BFGS-B", ...)` keeping ρ in a safe interval (e.g. `[-0.2, 0.0]` or a λ-aware bound), and/or clip τ to a small positive floor (`np.maximum(tau, 1e-10)`) before taking the log. Add an acceptance test asserting the fitted log-likelihood is finite and that no `log(τ)` of a non-positive value occurs.
> 
> Two smaller notes: (1) for the `score_matrix` criterion, the τ-corrected grid must be normalized (Σ ≠ 1 before normalization because τ perturbs the independent-Poisson product AND the grid is truncated at `max_goals`) — make sure "equals the product of marginals when ρ=0" is asserted against the *normalized* truncated marginals, not the raw scipy pmf, or the approx will fail. (2) Confirm `_prepare` drops `<NA>`-goal rows via `.isna()` on the `Int64` columns *before* `.astype(int)`, since casting `Int64` `<NA>` to a numpy int array raises.
> 
> The rest is well-grounded: the loader schema you cite is accurate, the seeded-RNG sampler contract correctly mirrors `FeatureSampler` (`np.random.default_rng`), and the additive `PoissonConfig` edit fits the `AppConfig` pattern. Heads-up on the shared `config.py` edit with the sibling Elo issue (#5) — both append a field to `AppConfig` and a new import, so expect a trivial merge conflict there; keep the edit minimal and additive as noted.
issues.title: feat: Dixon-Coles bivariate-Poisson estimator (attack/defence abilities + scoreline sampler)

cycles.scope: Elo engine: compute World Football Elo chronologically from martj42 (K by match importance, G margin multiplier, home_adv = 0 for neutral). Dixon-Coles bivariate Poisson with attack/defence strengths, home advantage, rho low-score correction, exponential time-decay. Both fit on historical data → emit per-team, per-date ratings + features for downstream models. Seed WC2026 live state from history.

cycles.name: Feature Assembly

issues.title: feat: static confederation map + FIFA-ranking fallback resolver + [features_build] config

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T22:00:15Z):
> The alignment contract this cycle depends on — the chronologically sorted frame being positionally 1:1 with EloResult.match_diffs — hinges on sort_chronological reproducing the Elo engine's sort byte-for-byte. But the plan has you declare a *new* sort-key constant in timeaware.py rather than reuse the engine's. Two independent literals will silently drift if elo.py's sort key ever changes, and the only symptom is leaked/misaligned features downstream. Fix: promote elo.py's private _SORT_COLS to a public name (e.g. elo.SORT_COLS) and import it here instead of re-declaring the list; replicate the full transform exactly — assign _d=pd.to_datetime(df['DATE'], errors='coerce'), sort_values(SORT_COLS, kind='stable', na_position='last'), drop _d, reset_index(drop=True). Add a test that runs a constructed frame through both the engine's ordering and sort_chronological and asserts row-for-row equality, so any future divergence fails loudly here rather than only in #9's runtime alignment guard. Separately, make the reference date for recent_form's decay explicit: anchor age_days to the *current* match date (D − prior_match_date) so 'time-weighted' is unambiguous and testable.
issues.title: feat: time-aware recent-form, rest-days, and goal-difference helpers (no forward leakage)

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T22:00:17Z):
> Performance-as-correctness risk in the per-row composition. As specified, recent_form, goal_difference, and rest_days each scan the prefix rows[:before_idx] for the team, and build() invokes them for both teams on every match. Over the full martj42 history (~tens of thousands of internationals) that is O(N²) prefix scans — a single build can run for many minutes and will dominate Cycle-5 backtests that rebuild repeatedly, with no runtime bound in the acceptance criteria to catch it. Root cause: re-deriving each team's prior matches from scratch on every row. Fix: make one chronological pass that maintains per-team incremental state (a rolling deque of recent played results + running goal-diff + last-played date), or build a per-team appearance index dict[team] -> list[(idx, date, points, goal_diff)] once and have the helpers take a windowed slice (O(window)) instead of an O(before_idx) scan. Keep the pure leaf helpers for the #8 leakage unit tests, but route build() through the indexed path so there is one tested code path rather than a fast production path diverging from the tested-but-slow helpers. Minor: MatchFeatures labels are typed int | None while inputs are nullable Int64 — convert pd.NA to None explicitly so the dataclass round-trips (the frame can keep <NA>).
issues.title: feat: core per-match feature assembler over martj42 history (features/build.py)

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T22:00:18Z):
> The plan is right to build over the full chronologically-sorted history and then filter to WC2026 rows — that preserves correct before_idx (no-leakage) semantics, and the explicit 'do NOT run the builder on the WC2026 subset alone' guard is the correct call. But there is a concrete gap: the assembler's MatchFeatures / output frame exposes no tournament, stage, or match-id column, so there is no key to select the WC2026 rows after building. Do not re-derive the subset by joining on (date, home, away) — neutral-site duplicates and same-day fixtures can collide and silently mis-select. Fix: compute the mask positionally — sort_chronological(df)['TOURNAMENT'] == 'FIFA World Cup' is row-for-row aligned with the output frame, so apply that boolean mask by position; alternatively coordinate with the assembler to pass through a non-covariate 'tournament' identifier column (metadata, not a feature, so it stays inside the football-only allow-list). Add a test asserting a WC2026 fixture's row carries pre-match form/rest/Elo that reflects the team's non-WC prior matches, proving the full-history build (not a WC-only build) produced it. The no-key martj42 path and the key-gated live path matching the verified TournamentState(played, remaining_group_fixtures, standings) shape are otherwise well-specified.
issues.title: feat: WC2026 current-state feature frame (no-key martj42 schedule + key-gated live adapter)
feat: features package public API, exports, and no-key integration smoke test

cycles.name: Model Training & Comparison

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T23:47:07Z):
> Solid foundation task — encoding, target derivation, and config wiring are well-specified. One concrete ordering footgun to pin down in `build_dataset`: the feature frame from `build_features` appends unplayed WC2026 fixtures with `home_goals`/`away_goals` as `<NA>`, and `sort_chronological` places them LAST (`na_position="last"`). If `chronological_split` (last `floor(n*test_size)` rows) runs BEFORE `played_only`, the entire test slice will be these NA-target fixtures, which then get dropped → an empty or near-empty test set. Make the acceptance criterion explicit: `build_dataset` must apply `played_only` FIRST, then `chronological_split` on the played-only frame, so both train and test contain only played matches. Add a test asserting `len(test) > 0` and `test['home_goals'].notna().all()` on a frame that includes unplayed rows. Also confirm `chronological_split` enforces `train.date.max() <= test.date.min()` on string ISO dates — string comparison is only safe if dates are zero-padded `YYYY-MM-DD` (they are, per `_date_str`), but assert it rather than relying on lexical order implicitly.
issues.title: feat: time-aware match dataset utility + Hybrid/OrderedLogit config models

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T23:47:08Z):
> Goal-based hybrid design is sound and reusing `simulation.poisson.score_matrix` for the W/D/L simplex is the right call (it normalizes via `mat/mat.sum()`, so triangles sum to 1 with non-zero draw mass). Two concrete corrections for the confederation encoding:
> 
> 1. The implementation note says to use a fixed ordering from `CONFEDERATION_MAP`, but that dict maps team→confederation, not a category order. The fixed category list you want is the `CONFEDERATIONS` tuple in `features/confederation.py` (`('UEFA','CONMEBOL','CAF','AFC','CONCACAF','OFC')`). The design-matrix columns `home_confederation`/`away_confederation` already CONTAIN confederation strings, so one-hot them against `CONFEDERATIONS` — not against `CONFEDERATION_MAP`.
> 
> 2. `resolve_ranking` returns `confederation=None` for unknown teams, so `home_confederation`/`away_confederation` can be `None`. A `None` must encode to an all-zero one-hot row (handled deterministically), not a NaN column — otherwise the 'no NaN columns' criterion breaks at WC2026 inference time.
> 
> Also: `build_features` adds a `tournament` metadata column that is NOT in `FEATURE_COLUMNS`. `build_design_matrix` should select an explicit allow-list of numeric + encoded columns rather than 'drop targets/identity', otherwise the object-dtype `tournament` column leaks in as a non-numeric/NaN column.
issues.title: feat: Groll-style RF/GBM goal-based hybrid model (primary predictor)

issues.comments:
## Pre-fetched Issue Comments

**SilvioBaratto** (2026-06-23T23:47:09Z):
> Lazy-importing `OrderedModel` inside `fit` and documenting this as the W/D/L-only fallback are both correct. Two concrete implementation hazards to bake into acceptance:
> 
> 1. `add_targets` produces `y_outcome` as nullable pandas `Int64`. statsmodels `OrderedModel` expects a plain numeric/categorical endog; passing `Int64` can raise or misbehave during the BFGS fit. Cast to a plain `np.int_`/`int` array (or `pd.Categorical` with ordered=True) before `OrderedModel(endog=..., exog=...)`. After `played_only`, `y_outcome` is guaranteed non-null, so the cast is safe.
> 
> 2. `OrderedModel` parameterizes the intercept via its cut-point thresholds — do NOT prepend a constant/intercept column to `exog` (statsmodels raises on a constant in exog for OrderedModel). `exog = X[config.features]` with `config.features=['elo_diff']` is exactly right; just ensure no `add_constant` sneaks in.
> 
> 3. For the monotonicity test, also guard the degenerate edge: with very few played rows or quasi-perfect separation, BFGS may not converge and coefficients blow up. Since this is the 'too few played rows' fallback, add a focused test on a small-but-separable synthetic frame and assert the fit produces finite coefficients.
issues.title: feat: Elo-diff ordered logit secondary model (statsmodels)
feat: models package no-key integration smoke test (hybrid + ordered logit + baseline)

cycles.scope: Groll-style RF/GBM hybrid: random forest or gradient boosting on Poisson abilities + Elo + covariates (arXiv 1806.03208). Elo-diff ordered-logit (secondary/fallback from statsmodels). Keep legacy SVM/RF/NB classifiers for backtest baseline comparison. Train with time-aware splits (no shuffle). All models must pass 250 existing tests; add new tests for hybrid + ordered-logit.

cycles.name: Evaluation, Simulation & Integration
cycles.scope: Extend tournament simulator: ingest played WC2026 results as fixed, simulate remaining group matches (correct tiebreak rules: goal-diff, goals-for, head-to-head, coin-flip), resolve Round of 32 from standings, simulate knockout (extra time λ×0.33 → penalty coin-flip), run ~100k Monte Carlo → full title odds + per-round advancement for all 48 teams. Historical odds scraper (backtest baseline: de-vig WC2014/18/22 from archived sources, cache to CSV). Evaluation metrics: RPS (primary), log-loss, Brier on time-aware splits. Config: add [poisson], [elo], [rf], [odds], [live] sections (Pydantic + TOML). CLI: new commands fetch-live, build-features, train-hybrid, backtest, forecast (live title odds) + existing bracket/PNG.
